hinge: This item is poorly formed with a garbled stem. Non-inflammatory bilateral parotid enlargement (sialadenosis) shows acinar hypertrophy histologically, while chronic sialadenitis shows acinar atrophy and leukocyte infiltrate. The intended answer appears to target sialadenosis with acinar hypertrophy as the biopsy finding.

Median rhomboid glossitis is a form of chronic erythematous candidiasis of the midline posterior dorsal tongue, strongly associated with Candida, often with kissing contact against the palate under a complete denture.

After a needlestick from an HIV-positive source, immediate first aid (wash) is followed by urgent occupational assessment and post-exposure prophylaxis started as soon as possible, ideally within hours.
In RPD design, the mandibular minor connector joins the major connector at a rigid butt-type junction for force transmission without a movable articulation.
Wits appraisal measures the linear distance between projections of points A and B onto the functional occlusal plane, not the anatomic occlusal plane, to assess sagittal jaw discrepancy.

Dental unit waterline biofilm thrives where water sits unused. Preventing stagnation through regular flushing, anti-retraction valves, and chemical treatment is the key control measure against biofilm accumulation.

Cross-infection is the transmission of infectious agents between patients, staff, or the environment during healthcare delivery, including colleague-to-colleague spread via contaminated instruments, surfaces, or aerosols.

In one-stage implant protocols, a transmucosal healing abutment (often dome-shaped) is placed at surgery so soft tissue heals around an open emergence profile, shaping peri-implant mucosa until definitive restoration.
Night sweats plus hemoptysis/blood-tinged sputum are classic red flags for pulmonary tuberculosis. TB is the working diagnosis to suspect until proven otherwise among the listed options.

Clinically, metal impression trays should be cleaned then heat-sterilized in an autoclave. Among the limited and poor options, thermal disinfection (hot water/thermal washer-disinfector pathway) is better than glutaraldehyde. None are ideal per strict clinical standard.

Conventional MTA formulations include bismuth oxide as the radiopacifier so the material is visible on radiographs. Calcium silicate is the hydraulic base and NaOCl is an irrigant, not a radiopacity additive.

On electronic apex locators, the red zone indicates the file tip has passed beyond the apical foramen. Green typically marks the apical constriction and readings short of the apex stay coronal to that landmark.

Osteoid osteoma classically causes night pain that is dramatically relieved by NSAIDs/aspirin due to prostaglandin mediation. Fibroma and other listed lesions do not share this aspirin-responsive pain pattern.

Dependent pitting edema of the lower legs in a patient with cardiac history indicates right-sided congestive heart failure with fluid retention. Infective endocarditis and hypertension alone do not produce this dependent edema sign.

For fixed orthodontic appliances, the Charter brushing method angles bristles toward the occlusal aspect of brackets and uses vibratory strokes to clean under wires and around hardware better than the Bass technique alone.

Immediate latex rubber-dam allergy is a Type I IgE-mediated hypersensitivity presenting with urticaria, bronchospasm, or anaphylaxis. Type IV is delayed contact dermatitis from rubber processing chemicals.

Bald red atrophic tongue with angular cheilitis and crusting suggests nutritional deficiency anemia (iron, B12, folate). Order CBC and related labs rather than assuming a purely local infectious cause.
Masseter, temporalis, and medial pterygoid are jaw elevators. The digastric (with lateral pterygoid and suprahyoid muscles) depresses and opens the mandible.

A short-term interim RPD after a single extraction is typically acrylic-based without cast rests. Rests are features of definitive cast frameworks — a provisional flipper-style prosthesis uses no rests.

Bilateral exophthalmos with systemic signs including hypertension and thyrotoxic features points to Graves disease (autoimmune hyperthyroidism with orbitopathy). Hashimoto and hypothyroidism lack this classic proptosis pattern; tumor is typically unilateral.

Vertical root fracture is confirmed definitively by direct visualization via surgical exploration/flap or extraction inspection. CBCT and multiple PAs can support suspicion but surgical exploration is the gold standard for confirmation.

Large open interproximal contacts are cleaned most effectively with an interdental brush sized to the embrasure. Standard floss and superfloss work better in tight contacts; a conventional toothbrush alone does not clean mid-embrasure plaque.

Recession-related dentin hypersensitivity localizes to exposed cervical root dentin near the CEJ, where open dentinal tubules face thermal and tactile stimuli. Mid-root is usually covered by gingiva or cementum.

Carisolv is a chemomechanical caries-removal system using NaOCl-based gel with amino acids that softens infected dentin for selective hand excavation without rotary cutting as the primary caries removal method.
Significant renal impairment is a classic contraindication for fluoride varnish because reduced renal clearance raises systemic fluoride load and increases toxicity risk.
Abruptly cutting off a patient's personal narrative solely to demand the chief complaint violates treating the patient as a person — failing to respect, listen, and practice person-centered care.

Silver obturation points corrode in tissue fluids. Corrosion products are toxic to periapical tissues and are associated with inflammation and treatment failure, which led to their abandonment in modern endodontics.

Transillumination with a fiber-optic light helps visualize crack lines as they block light transmission through tooth structure. While pain on release is the clinical sign, transillumination is the direct confirmatory chairside test.

Mild bilateral tuberosity undercuts that aid denture retention are managed by relieving the denture base rather than surgically removing both undercuts. Aggressive bilateral reduction is reserved for cases where insertion path or trauma makes undercuts harmful.

Electrosurgery/electrocautery can generate electromagnetic interference that may affect pacemaker function and trigger arrhythmia. Intraoral radiographs, panoramic imaging, and high-speed handpieces are not the classic pacemaker EMI culprits.
